with non-trivial circular references fails in 1.11.2","user":{"login":"DrChainsaw","id":36208552,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjM2MjA4NTUy","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/36208552?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/DrChainsaw","html_url":"https://github.com/DrChainsaw","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/DrChainsaw/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/DrChainsaw/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/DrChainsaw/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/DrChainsaw/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/DrChainsaw/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/DrChainsaw/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/DrChainsaw/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/DrChainsaw/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/DrChainsaw/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":3,"created_at":"2024-12-07T16:21:19Z","updated_at":"2024-12-20T23:24:16Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","sub_issues_summary":{"total":0,"completed":0,"percent_completed":0},"active_lock_reason":null,"body":"Not sure if this is just a symptom of deepcopy not having much in terms of guaranteed behaviour, but it is no longer consistent with serialize => deserialize.

I tried to recreate it with a simpler structure (e.g. just Arrays which contain themselves) but couldn't. This is the most stripped down version I could create:

```julia
struct ProblemStruct{T}
    label::String # Just helps a bit when debugging
    inputs::T
end

using Serialization

let 
    problem = ProblemStruct("problem", ProblemStruct[])

    next = ProblemStruct("next", [problem])
    push!(problem.inputs, next) # Make a circular reference

    println("Original:")
    @show problem === next.inputs[1]
    @show problem.inputs[1] === next

    problemc, nextc = deepcopy((problem, next))
    println("\ndeepcopy:")
    @show problemc === nextc.inputs[1]
    @show problemc.inputs[1] === nextc

    println("\nserde:")
    problemds, nextds = let 
        io = PipeBuffer()
        serialize(io, (problem, next))
        deserialize(io)
    end
    @show problemds === nextds.inputs[1]
    @show problemds.inputs[1] === nextds

end;
```

Prints the following in Julia 1.11.2:
```
Original:
problem === next.inputs[1] = true    
problem.inputs[1] === next = true    

deepcopy:
problemc === nextc.inputs[1] = false 
problemc.inputs[1] === nextc = true  

serde:
problemds === nextds.inputs[1] = true
problemds.inputs[1] === nextds = true
```

And this in Julia 1.10.7:
```
Original:
problem === next.inputs[1] = true
problem.inputs[1] === next = true

deepcopy:
problemc === nextc.inputs[1] = true
problemc.inputs[1] === nextc = true

serde:
problemds === nextds.inputs[1] = true
problemds.inputs[1] === nextds = true
```

Fwiw: I can work around the problem on my side with the following:
```julia
function Base.deepcopy_internal(v::Vector{ProblemStruct}, stackdict::IdDict)
    v in keys(stackdict) && return stackdict[v]
    newv = similar(v)
    stackdict[v] = newv
    for (i, vi) in zip(eachindex(newv), v)
        newv[i] = Base.deepcopy_internal(vi, stackdict)
    end
    return newv
end
```

The issue is in https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/blob/083b24eaa4d5f5a0711791f16109e93d52cd6834/base/deepcopy.jl#L127 from commit https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/commit/909bceae8a60969dfceba4924142e5efee459d47

The deepcopy_internal construction should register the new copied item in the stackdict before recursing to the children. Failing that, we need to check again when ascending from the stack. So the fix is
```
julia> @eval Base begin
       function deepcopy_internal(x::Array{T, N}, stackdict::IdDict) where {T, N}
           if haskey(stackdict, x)
               return stackdict[x]::typeof(x)
           end
           copied = $(Expr(:new, :(Array{T, N}), :(deepcopy_internal(x.ref, stackdict)), :(x.size)))
           #we need to check again, in case that we just recursed back to x
           get!(stackdict, x, copied)
           end
           end
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/2557875803/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

{"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56435","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56435/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56435/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56435/events","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56435","id":2632546131,"node_id":"I_kwDOABkWpM6c6XdT","number":56435,"title":"Type confusion with jl_genericmemory_to_string","user":{"login":"bbrehm","id":29487190,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI5NDg3MTkw","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/29487190?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/bbrehm","html_url":"https://github.com/bbrehm","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/bbrehm/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/bbrehm/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/bbrehm/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/bbrehm/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/bbrehm/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/bbrehm/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/bbrehm/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/bbrehm/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/bbrehm/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2024-11-04T11:25:09Z","updated_at":"2024-11-04T11:25:09Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"```
julia> function foo()
       v=UInt8[0x41 for i=1:801];
       popfirst!(v)
       vv=reshape(v, (800, 1))
       vvv = reshape(vv, (800,))
       s = String(vvv)
       Ref(s)
       end
foo (generic function with 1 method)

julia> foo()
Base.RefValue{String}(UInt8[0x41, 0x41, 0x41, 0x41, 0x41, 0x41, 0x41, 0x41, 0x41, 0x41  …  0x41, 0x41, 0x00, 0x41, 0x41, 0x41, 0x41, 0x41, 0x41, 0x41])
```
From discourse https://discourse.julialang.org/t/does-julia-have-efficient-move-semantics/122165/33

I think the issue is that `jl_genericmemory_to_string` labors under the mistaken assumption that only a string can be the owner of the memory if `how == 3`.

As a further issue, I am quite unhappy with `jl_genericmemory_to_string` mutating the Memory's `length` field: Memory is supposed to have constant length. If another array, especially a non-Vector, uses the same memory, then it's not supposed to have its size changed under it!

And mutating the memory doesn't really help, there could be other jl_genericmemory_slice to the same memory.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56435/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56435/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}
